title: Tech Disruptors: SailPoint’s Mills on Governance for AI Agents
summary: The governance landscape for AI agents is evolving amid new developments around agentic workflows, says SailPoint President Matt Mills. He joins Bloomberg Intelligence analyst Mandeep Singh on this episode of the Tech Disruptors podcast. The conversation also explores how agentic AI could reshape identity governance, from internal functions and product capabilities to potential shifts in pricing models and customer adoption.

title: China Bets Big on Green Hydrogen: Analyst Reaction (Podcast)
summary: China is emerging as the standout exception in the global hydrogen market. While many countries are scaling back ambitions, Beijing has set a new target to increase green hydrogen production eightfold by 2030, a level that could make it the world’s largest producer and account for roughly half of BloombergNEF’s projected global output that year. The strategy is about more than decarbonization. China sees green hydrogen as a way to strengthen energy security, reduce reliance on imported oil and g

title: Ondas to Buy Military Drone Maker DZYNE for $875.8 Million
summary: Ondas Inc. agreed to buy autonomous aircraft maker DZYNE Technologies in an $875.8 million cash-and stock-deal that positions Ondas as a full-service autonomous defense technology platform.

title: Memory Chipmaker SK Hynix Kicks Off $28 Billion US Listing
summary:
SK Hynix kicked off the formal marketing process for its US listing on Monday, as the South Korean chipmaker looks to capitalize on surging investor demand for the high-flying memory-chip sector.
Bailey Lipschultz, Bloomberg News Senior Equities Reporter, joined Bloomberg's Paul Sweeney to highlight the formal marketing process for SK Hynix and its US listing. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Tech Giants Drive Stock Gains in AI Trade Revival: Markets Wrap
summary: A rally in several technology powerhouses lifted stocks amid speculation that the artificial-intelligence trade that has powered the bull market has more room to run.

title: Ardian’s Santé Cie Seeks to Enter French Restructuring Procedure
summary: Santé Cie SAS is seeking to enter French restructuring proceedings after some of its cash assets were seized as part of an investigation from the authorities into alleged corruption and money laundering.

title: Sports-Related Deals on the Rise, Says William Blair’s IB Head
summary: Acquisitions in the sports industry are set to increase as investors leverage a slew of different approaches to the fast-growing sector, according to William Blair & Co.’s head of investment banking.

title: Dental Chain Owner Aspen Drops WellNow Sale as Lenders Lawyer Up
summary: The Aspen Group has indicated it’s no longer moving ahead with a planned sale of its urgent care clinic chain WellNow, after seeking buyers amid an effort to address a looming debt deadline that’s also prompted its lenders to retain lawyers.

title: Peace Deal Doesn’t Restore Pre-War Situation, ECB’s Schnabel Says
summary: Peace efforts in the Middle East have helped bring energy prices down rapidly but haven’t restored the position the world was in before hostilities erupted, according to European Central Bank Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel.

title: The Restaurant Economy Doesn’t Look K-Shaped
summary: It looks like a broad based boom.

title: Boston Airport Borrows $812 Million for Revamp as Traffic Soars
summary: The busiest airport in New England is tapping the municipal bond market to remodel its facilities and keep up with passenger growth.

title: Hungary Sells €3 Billion in Post-Orban Eurobond Return
summary: Hungary sold €3 billion ($3.4 billion) at its first international bond issuance since Viktor Orban’s ouster in April’s election triggered a plunge in borrowing costs.

title: Belgium Fumes After FIFA Clears Balogun Following Trump Call
summary: FIFA's decision to overturn US striker Folarin Balogun's World Cup suspension — after an intervention from Donald Trump — has caused fury in Belgium. Max Ramsay explains why the reversal is overshadowing their crucial match against Team USA. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trans Mountain 3.0 Revives Indigenous Pipeline Ownership Push
summary: Canada’s announcement last week that Trans Mountain Corp. will build a 1 million-barrel-a-day oil pipeline along the government-owned company’s existing Alberta-to-British Columbia route is rekindling Indigenous interest in acquiring a stake in the company.

title: Welltower Seeks at Least $492 Million From Canada Bond Sale
summary: Welltower Inc. is looking to raise at least C$700 million ($492 million) in a two-part bond deal, the latest US firm to tap investors north of the border.

title: Nvidia Server Delay Report Sends Asian Tech Stocks Sliding
summary: Asian technology stocks slumped after a report that Nvidia Corp.’s next-generation AI server rack system has been delayed by more than a year due to manufacturing difficulties.

title: James McGinniss on Battery Solutions For Energy Prices
summary: During a severe heat wave in New York City, power prices have surged to historic levels. James McGinnis, CEO and co-founder of David Energy, explained that while demand strains the grid annually, new technologies are helping to mitigate the impact. He spoke on July 3, 2026. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Private Equity's Next Sports Play
summary: Wall Street is making a bigger bet on sports. William Blair's acquisition of Inner Circle Sports underscores why investors see enormous opportunity in pro teams, women's sports and college athletics. Inner Circle Sports Founder Rob Tillis and William Blair's Global Head of Investment Banking Matt Zimmer join Open Interest to explain what's driving soaring franchise values and why they believe the industry's biggest growth is still ahead. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Summer Box Office Builds Momentum
summary: The summer box office is heating up, and more momentum could be on the way. Daniel Loria, Senior Vice President at The Boxoffice Company, says two major July releases -- Universal's The Odyssey and Sony's Spider-Man: Brand New Day -- could keep audiences coming to theaters. He joined Bloomberg TV with his outlook. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Argentina Shuns Markets for Now, Sees Investment Grade by 2031
summary: President Javier Milei’s administration is targeting investment-grade status for Argentina by the end of a potential second term in 2031, while continuing to shun international debt markets.

title: AI Job Disruption Has Come for Ireland's Technology Sector
summary: The country benefited from a jobs boom after attracting US multinationals, but some of these roles are being cut in the name of AI efficiency.

title: Trump Sparks Soccer Red Card Drama
summary: A stunning red-card reversal has sparked a global soccer controversy after President Trump weighed in on Folarin Balogun's suspension. Bloomberg's Randall Williams joins the show to talk about whether the decision was fair, if slow-motion review distorts intent, and why the ruling could create a dangerous precedent for the World Cup. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Hedge Fund Veterans Are Accelerating a Major Shift in Development Finance
summary: A group of hedge fund veterans is helping drive a fundamental shift in how to generate financing for developing nations.

title: Ontario, Alberta Pitch Cross-Canada Oil Pipeline to Ease US Reliance
summary: Alberta and Ontario proposed a crude oil pipeline and energy corridor across Canada as a way of reducing the country’s dependence on the US.

title: Jessica Noviskis on Market Uncertainty Surrounding AI Rally
summary: Marquette Associates OCIO Portfolio Strategist Jessica Noviskis, discusses the current market environment and how Marquette Associates is investing. She speak with Guy Johnson on July 3, 2026. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Germany to Sweeten Terms of Gas Plant Auction to Draw More Bids
summary: Germany will lift the maximum amount it will pay developers in upcoming auctions for gas-fired power plants as it seeks to attract more bids for urgently needed back-up power capacity.

title: Quant Funds Extend Worst Run Since 2023 as Momentum Swings
summary: A violent rotation in the underbelly of a bullish stock market is extending the worst run for quantitative hedge funds since 2023.

title: Professional Soccer in the US Has Been Waiting for This Moment Says Shawn Quill
summary: Shawn Quill, Sports Industry Leader at KPMG US, discusses the economic impact of the World Cup and how it will affect the growth of soccer in the US long-term. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Corn, Soybeans Touch Highest in a Month as Traders Watch Weather
summary: Soybean and corn futures jumped Monday as traders turned their attention to weather concerns looming over global crops.

title: Bank of Canada Surveys Show War Boosted Inflation Expectations, Investment
summary: The war in Iran caused a spike in Canadian inflation expectations and is leading the country’s oil producers to boost their investment and production plans.

title: BMO Capital Markets' Jennifer Lee on Labor Market, Inflation
summary: BMO Capital Markets Senior Economist Jennifer Lee discusses the latest jobs report and how the Iran conflict could be distorting inflation. The interview occurred on July 3, 2026. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Two Millennium Trading Pods Made About $3.7 Billion Last Month
summary: Two teams focused on trading index changes at Millennium Management made billions of dollars between them in June, lifting the multistrategy hedge fund’s monthly gains.
